project.title: Labeled quantitative proteomics dataset of optogenetics induced axon regeneration
project.projectDescription: This labeled quantitative proteomics dataset was collected from a transgenic channel rhodopsin mouse model (Chr2) subjected to light stimulation after traumatic optic nerve crush (ONC) was performed. Mouse models expressing channel rhodopsin were subjected to therapeutic light stimulation promoting axon regeneration of retinal ganglion cell (RGC) axons post optic nerve crush. Experimental mice and wild type control mice were euthanized, and optic nerves were collected. A protein extraction was carried out by careful mincing of the tissue in extraction buffer (TEAB, NaCl and SDS). Protein amount normalization across samples was achieved using dot blot densitometry using ImageJ software. Samples were labelled using a modified 16plex TMT (Tandem Mass Tag) kit for quantification after overnight trypsin digestion. Untargeted liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry was performed on an Easy-nLC 1000 liquid chromatograph coupled to a QExactive mass spectrometer (LC-MS/MS). Data analysis was performed using Proteome Discoverer 2.5.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Animals were euthanized and optic nerves were collected. There were 12 experimental conditions and three biological replicates for each condition for a total of 36 nerves. Protein extraction was carried out by mincing of tissue in TEAB, NaCl and SDS. Dot blot densitometry was used to estimate and normalize protein amounts across all samples. Protein was trypsin digested overnight and samples were labelled with TMT label reagents. Each set of biological replicates was labelled with 12 labelling reagents (modified from a 16Plex Thermo Scientific kit) and combined into three separate tubes for mass spec analysis. Samples were separated with an Easy-Spray HPLC column (Thermo Scientific) on an Easy-nLC 1000 instrument (Thermo Scientific). Untargeted mass spectrometry was performed on a QExactive mass spectrometer (LC MS-MS).
project.dataProcessingProtocol: All raw mass spectrometry data files were analyzed using Proteome Discoverer 2.5. The mouse proteome was downloaded from UniProt and used as the target database. Max missed cleavage site was set to 2 and minimum peptide length to 6. Precursor Mass Tolerance was set to 10ppm and Fragment Mass Tolerance to 0.02 Da. Post-translational modifications included oxidation, acetylation, carbamidomethylation and TMTpro. Normalization was set to total peptide amount and confidence to high.
